name: Bob Crewe
description: American songwriter, dancer, singer, manager, and record producer (1931–2014)
abstract: Robert Stanley Crewe (November 12, 1930 – September 11, 2014) was an American singer, songwriter, record producer, dancer and manager, best known mainly for co-writing and producing a string of Top 10 singles with Bob Gaudio for the 1960s pop rock band The Four Seasons. As a songwriter, his most successful songs include "Silhouettes" (co…

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Calello
about_quotes.evidence: Bob Crewe ultimately became the producer of The Four Seasons... And he asked us if we wanted to be his studio band... Now being taught by Bob Crewe... I was writing arrangements for nothing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles Calello interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 21, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Joel Dorn
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I love — sometimes there are certain songs if you’re in — you can pan things from left to right, like Frankie Valli, the Four Seasons records. >> [INTERVIEWEE] Oh Bob Crewe.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joel Dorn interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Pallo
about_quotes.evidence: And that was at Phillips Recording Studio which Bob Crewe was the producer I remember that. We did eight hours one day
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Pallo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2007
